text: Communication Between Teacher and Parent to Respond to Changing Society So that Children Possess Islamic Values Adequately through Islamic Educational Institutions, Especially BA Aisyiyah Gumiwang This study deals with the problem of communication of religious messages as an effort to apply Islamic values at this time in which society undergoes many changes and social norms keep on shifting. In fact the kids at BA Aisyiyah Gumiwang need a heavy religious communication so that they will acquire an abundant number of Islamic values. This research uses qualitative descriptive approach. Data were collected by applying deep interview methods towards the school manager, teachers, and students. Some data were also collected through observational research and documents of the school activities. The communication of religious messages employed by teachers in BA Aisyiyah Gumiwang School is applied based on several psychological theories related to the children such as: 1. Social Cognitive Theory, where children learn the things they see (by way of example) from other people like teachers, parents and others, 2. Cognitive Development Theory which is divided into several levels: that children enter the world through their sensory-motor level then enter preoperational stage and carry out concretaive activities in the concrete operational stage, at the end of which stage they have reached formal operational stage, 3. Constructivism, where the children are taught to learn actively or construct their knowledge by themselves. The results showed that the teacher behavior in teaching the religion as one part of pendidikan kejiwaan Nasional (psychological education) at BA Aisyiyah Gumiwang School by utilizing scaffolding by implementing teacher modeling by performing many activities, implementing the student's education and worship by reciting religious songs and performing prayer regularly. Hence, it can be seen that many models are applied at the school and it is used in order to train the children to follow Islamic education sufficiently so that the children will gain sufficient number of religious values and can face many problems they face.
headline: Fully Human Written
